<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 9 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- llms.txt and context.md are generated outputs consumed by AI tooling — their presence at root signals this project is designed for AI-assisted workflows from the start.
- README.md and image assets (Rex.png, Rex-F.png) are documentation artifacts; keeping them at root makes the project immediately navigable for new contributors.
- The vitest.config.ts at root governs monorepo-wide test execution, centralizing test configuration rather than duplicating it across packages.
- Zero cohesion and coupling are expected for a root config zone — these files share a location, not a runtime dependency, so the metrics are not actionable.
- Generated AI context files (llms.txt, context.md) living at the monorepo root rather than inside a package directory suggests they are intended for cross-package consumption, which is architecturally appropriate.
- Absence from the cross-zone import matrix confirms monorepo-config files are never imported by source packages, maintaining a clean separation between project configuration and executable code.
- context.md and llms.txt at the monorepo root coexist with package-level .sourcevision/CONTEXT.md and llms.txt files. Without documented precedence rules, AI consumers may use stale root-level context rather than the current per-package analysis output, silently operating on outdated codebase state.
- Document the precedence hierarchy between root-level context.md/llms.txt and package-level .sourcevision/CONTEXT.md/llms.txt. Add a comment or README note clarifying which file is authoritative for AI tooling. Consider whether the root-level files should be generated from package-level files rather than maintained separately, to prevent drift.

</insights>
